description: 18th Street Ambrose was a major Chicago gang established in Pilsen in 1964 that expanded throughout the city and suburbs with peak power in the 1990s. The organization was heavily involved in PCP distribution and became one of the biggest drug distributors in Chicago before federal Operation Blue Water shut down their operation in 2001. Despite the devastating 2001 raid, Ambrose maintained a presence in certain Chicago neighborhoods including Marquette Park.

Black Gangster Disciples connected with the Ashland Vikings, a mostly Puerto Rican group, the Simon City Royals, a mostly white group and Ambrose, a mostly Mexican group. Black Gangster Disciples would pay Ambrose members to go out and kill one of their enemies... The gangs also trafficked weapons and drugs together and this built a deep connection between Ambrose, Gangster Disciples and Simon City Royals.

According to court documents in the case of the People vs. Rodriguez, Joyce Rodriguez was convicted of driving a car used in a shooting at 16th and Fairfield in the North Lawndale neighborhood against the Satan Disciples.
Both Ambrose and Satan Disciples would become part of the Folks alliance in the late 70s/early 80s and would even form the 'ATSD' which stood for 'Ambrose Two Six and Satan Disciples.'

Two Two Boys and Ambrose became very close in the midst of all this which turned them into 'cousins' on the streets. They remained tight through the 2000s.
